name: Castellote and Mas de las Mat
name_original: Castellote & Sierra de los Caballos - circular desde Molinos
description: This challenging 32.7-mile road cycling route from Molinos explores the rugged Sierra de los Caballos, offering dramatic views and significa
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Molinos are characterized by a mountainous landscape featuring significant elevation changes and numerous passes. The region offers extensive loop routes that traverse varied terrain. Road cyclists can expect challenging climbs and descents, often through remote areas. The routes frequently include mountain passes, providing a demanding experience for those seeking elevation. Best road cycling routes around Molinos The most popular road cycling route is Castellote and Mas de las Mat, a 32.7 miles (52.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 44 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain, making it a challenging ride. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Ejulve - Aliaga Maestrazgo Loop, a difficult 77.7 miles (125.0 km) path. This route offers a scenic loop through the Maestrazgo region. Local road cyclists also love the Puerto de Villarluengo – Mountain Pass loop from Ejulve, a 72.8 miles (117.2 km) trail leading through mountain passes, often completed in about 6 hours 21 minutes. Road cycling around Molinos is defined by mountain passes, extensive loops, and significant elevation changes. The network offers options primarily for moderate to advanced skill levels. The routes in Molinos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 250 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Molinos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes around Molinos?
A: Road cycling routes in Molinos are predominantly challenging, characterized by significant elevation changes and numerous mountain passes. The region offers options primarily for &lt;strong&gt;moderate to advanced skill levels&lt;/strong&gt;, with 15 out of 22 routes classified as difficult.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Molinos?
A: Molinos is known for its mountainous terrain and challenging climbs, making most routes suitable for experienced cyclists. There are &lt;strong&gt;no easy routes&lt;/strong&gt;, and only 7 out of 22 routes are classified as moderate. Beginners might find the elevation gains demanding.

Q: What kind of distances and durations can I expect on Molinos road cycling routes?
A: Routes vary significantly in length and duration. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e959239972/castellote-and-mas-de-las-mat" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellote and Mas de las Mat&lt;/a&gt; route is about 52.6 km (32.7 miles) and takes around 2 hours 44 minutes. Longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961402763/ejulve-aliaga-maestrazgo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ejulve - Aliaga Maestrazgo Loop&lt;/a&gt; stretch to 125 km (77.7 miles), requiring over 6 hours to complete.

Q: What are the best seasons for road cycling in Molinos?
A: The best seasons for road cycling in Molinos are typically &lt;strong&gt;spring and autumn&lt;/strong&gt;. During these times, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for sustained physical activity. Summer can be very hot, especially in the middle of the day, while winter may bring colder temperatures and potential snow at higher elevations.

Q: Are there any scenic road cycling routes with notable points of interest?
A: Many routes offer stunning scenery through the mountainous landscape. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961402763/ejulve-aliaga-maestrazgo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ejulve - Aliaga Maestrazgo Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides a scenic journey through the Maestrazgo region. You can also explore routes that pass by landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellote Village and Castle&lt;/a&gt; or the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de Crespol Gorge&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Molinos?
A: The terrain around Molinos is characterized by a &lt;strong&gt;mountainous landscape&lt;/strong&gt; with significant elevation changes. You can expect challenging climbs and descents, often through remote areas. Routes frequently include demanding mountain passes, offering a rigorous experience for those seeking elevation.

Q: Are there loop road cycling trails available in Molinos?
A: Yes, Molinos is well-suited for loop road cycling. The region offers &lt;strong&gt;extensive loop routes&lt;/strong&gt; that traverse varied terrain. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e961402763/ejulve-aliaga-maestrazgo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ejulve - Aliaga Maestrazgo Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31472608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Villarluengo – Mountain Pass loop from Ejulve&lt;/a&gt;, both providing circular journeys through the area.

Q: What are some notable attractions or landmarks to see near the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the cycling itself, the Molinos area offers several interesting attractions. You might consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5361244" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Santa María del Olivar&lt;/a&gt;, exploring the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357652" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellote Village and Castle&lt;/a&gt;, or experiencing the natural beauty of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5355841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estrecho de Crespol Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes pass close to these points of interest.

Q: What do other road cyclists say about their experience in Molinos?
A: The road cycling experience in Molinos is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;5.0 stars&lt;/strong&gt; from reviews. Cyclists often praise the challenging mountain passes, extensive loop routes, and the rewarding elevation changes that define the region's rides.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in the Molinos area?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;over 20 road cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; available around Molinos on komoot, offering a wide range of challenging options for cyclists looking to explore the mountainous terrain.

Q: Are there any routes that include significant mountain passes?
A: Yes, mountain passes are a defining feature of road cycling in Molinos. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31472608" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Villarluengo – Mountain Pass loop from Ejulve&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cristóbal Pass – Puerto de Majalinos loop from Gargallo&lt;/a&gt; are specifically designed to take you over these challenging and scenic ascents.

Q: What should I wear for road cycling in Molinos?
A: Given the significant elevation changes and potential for varied weather, it's advisable to wear &lt;strong&gt;layered cycling apparel&lt;/strong&gt;. This allows you to adjust to temperature fluctuations during climbs and descents. Always check the local weather forecast before heading out, and consider bringing a lightweight windbreaker or rain jacket.
tips: The Gallipén reservoir is a dam that collects the waters of the Guadalopillo river as it passes through the town of Berge.
Mas de las Matas is a small town in the region of Baix Aragó that is right at the foot of the Iberian System. Surrounded by limestone mountains such as the Sierra de los Caballos

faqs.question: Where does the route start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular one starting and ending in Molinos. While specific parking details aren't provided, Molinos, being the starting point, would be the most logical place to find parking. Look for public parking areas within the village.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult and requires a very good fitness level. The Sierra de los Caballos is characterized by significant elevation changes, including demanding climbs and descents, making it unsuitable for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: Given the arid mountain landscape, spring and autumn are generally the best times to cycle this route. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures and potentially less predictable road conditions in mountainous areas.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for cycling in Maestrazgo?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads and trails within the Maestrazgo region. However, if you plan to visit specific attractions like the Gallipuén Reservoir or the Grutas de Cristal, they may have their own entrance fees.

faqs.question: What are the road conditions and terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is rugged and mountainous, featuring an arid-looking series of parallel mountain ranges deeply cut by ravines. Expect significant elevation changes with demanding climbs and descents. While many routes in rural Spain are mostly paved, some segments, especially on more challenging rides, may be less well-maintained. You'll encounter winding roads and potentially tight turns.

faqs.question: What scenic highlights or landmarks can I expect to see along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience expansive views of the rugged Spanish interior. Notable highlights include the Gallipuén Reservoir, and passing through the settlements of Mas de las Matas and Castellote. The impressive Grutas de Cristal ('glass caves') near Molinos are also a prominent natural attraction in the area, though not directly on the cycling route, they are a worthwhile stop.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: This route is estimated to take approximately 2 hours and 44 minutes of pure cycling time. However, given its 'difficult' classification and the significant elevation gain, allow extra time for breaks, enjoying the scenery, and navigating challenging sections.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other official paths. You will find sections that overlap with the Camino del Maestrazgo-Bajo Aragón, Vuelta a la Atalaya y Castillo de Castellote, Ruta del Agua, Mas de las Matas, and Camino del Ruedo.

faqs.question: Is this route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so while dogs are generally allowed in many outdoor areas, bringing a dog on a demanding road cycling trip of this nature might not be practical or safe for the animal, especially given the potential for traffic and the physical exertion required. It's best suited for human cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route passes through or near settlements like Molinos, Mas de las Matas, and Castellote. These villages typically offer amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options, allowing you to refuel and rest.

faqs.question: What should I pack for cycling this route?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging nature and mountainous terrain, pack essentials like plenty of water, energy snacks, a repair kit for your racebike, appropriate cycling attire for varying temperatures, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A fully charged phone and a reliable navigation device are also highly recommended.
